summary: After the server thread publishes the modified skills to GitHub, sync the authoritative OpenClaw and Hermes skill repositories onto the local machine, then create fresh local OpenClaw and Hermes instances and validate strict self-hosted Community access on the local machine.

in_scope: Read the server publication result and consume the exact GitHub repos, branches, and commit SHAs.
Sync the authoritative OpenClaw and Hermes skill repositories from GitHub onto the local machine.
Prepare a supported local runtime surface on the user's own machine.
Create fresh local OpenClaw and Hermes instances.
Use the synced skills to connect both fresh local instances to Community.
Validate the updated self-hosted-first design end to end on the local machine.
out_of_scope: Treating server-side execution as local self-hosted validation.
Counting config-file presence as running-service proof.
Ignoring the server publication result and freelancing on stale local code.

design_conclusion: Strict self-hosted validation means the tested OpenClaw and Hermes instances must run on the user's own machine.
A local WSL or local Linux runtime on the user's own machine still counts as local self-hosted.
Formal local model config is the truth source.
community-agent.env and similar files are only snapshot or compatibility surfaces, not the sole truth source.
If formal local model config cannot be inherited or proven, the skill must fail closed.
prerequisite_from_server_thread: Do not start real implementation or validation until the server publication thread has returned the GitHub repo URLs, branches, and commit SHAs for both OpenClaw and Hermes.
Use those exact GitHub refs as the local sync baseline.

must_do: Sync from the authoritative GitHub refs produced by the server publication thread.
Use the local machine, not the community server host, as the place where the fresh agents run.
If native Windows is not a supported full live path, move execution into local WSL or another local Linux surface on this same machine.
must_not_do: Do not modify myproject or Community server.
Do not claim full live acceptance from preflight-only inheritance proof.
Do not proceed on stale local skill code if the GitHub publication step has not completed.

execution_order: Read the server publication result and capture the exact GitHub refs.
Inspect local repo state and sync the OpenClaw and Hermes skill repos safely from GitHub.
Record local repo paths, branches, and synced commit SHAs.
Prepare a supported local runtime surface on this machine.
Create a fresh local OpenClaw instance and a fresh local Hermes instance.
Use the synced skills to connect both instances to Community.
Run fresh live validation, fail-closed negative validation, and if possible dual live validation.

repo_sync: If the existing local checkout is dirty and a safe pull is unclear, create a clean sibling clone instead of force-resetting.
Record the final synced commit SHA for each skill line.
openclaw_local: Create a fresh local OpenClaw instance.
Prove it inherits runnable model config from the proper local truth source.
Start the real running service locally.
Validate identity, active webhook subscription, session or sync, recoverable local state, usable send route, canonical effect, and running-service model-config inheritance.
hermes_local: Create a fresh local Hermes instance.
Prove it inherits runnable model config from the proper local truth source.
Start the real running service locally.
Validate identity, active webhook subscription, session or sync, recoverable local state, usable send route, canonical effect, and running-service model-config inheritance.
negative_validation: Run a missing-formal-model-config fail-closed test for OpenClaw.
Run a missing-formal-model-config fail-closed test for Hermes.
Clearly distinguish preflight fail-closed from running-service readiness fail-closed.
dual_live: If both fresh local instances are running, validate OpenClaw to Hermes live flow.
Validate Hermes to OpenClaw live flow.
For both directions, prove send route, targeted inbound delivery, and canonical effect.

minimum_acceptance: The local machine is synced to the GitHub-published OpenClaw and Hermes skill refs.
Fresh local OpenClaw live onboarding is proven end to end.
Fresh local Hermes live onboarding is proven end to end.
Running-service model-config inheritance is proven for both lines.
Fail-closed negative behavior is proven for both lines.
Dual live validation is proven in both directions if both fresh instances can run locally.
deliverables: Repo sync summary with exact local repo paths, GitHub refs, and synced commit SHAs.
OpenClaw local implementation and validation summary.
Hermes local implementation and validation summary.
Structured evidence paths under a new codexlogs research-archive directory.
A final hard conclusion: fully_validated, partially_validated, or not_validated.
